event: Cocktail test revealed gonadotropin-releasing hormone deficiency and growth hormone deficiency
Stopped rGH therapy and began taking metformin
Began taking glipizide and long-acting insulin
Oxygen saturation decreased to 50% when sleeping
Advised to apply positive pressure ventilation
Poor compliance with positive pressure ventilation
Two-dimensional echocardiography revealed normal ventricle size and normal systolic function
Presented with dyspnea and loss of consciousness
Height and weight: 164 cm and 185.7 kg
Periorbicular cyanosis, respiratory distress, skin ulcers, and macular rash
Chest x-ray showed cardiomegaly and haziness in the right lung
Arterial blood gas analysis revealed pH of 6.9, PCO2 of 147, PO2 of 72.1, and HCO3 of 29.3
Admitted to ICU after intubation with synchronized intermittent mandatory ventilation
Aspartate aminotransferase/alanine aminotransferase of 51 U/L/59 U/L
Blood urea nitrogen/creatinine of 34.9 mg/dL/0.93 mg/dL
Chest computed tomography revealed bilateral pleural effusion and peribronchial opacity
Provided normal saline and noradrenalin for hemodynamic restoration, cefotaxime for septic shock, midazolam and fentanyl for sedation, and physiotherapy
Testing for any respiratory virus was negative
Transesophageal echocardiography revealed a dilated RV cavity, decreased RV systolic function, and pulmonary hypertension
Placed in a special ICU bed with a pressure-relieving mattress and a weighing machine
Added clindamycin and dressed his wounds daily
Ventilator mode was changed from volume control (VC) SIMV to pressure-control (PC) SIMV
Pulmonary congestion and pleural effusion did not improve
Adjusted target balance of input/output to -1,500–2,000 mL/day
Started enteral nutrition via a nasogastric tube
Fluids and electrolytes were corrected daily according to body weight
Oxygen was delivered at a high flow rate of 50 L/min
Fraction of inspired oxygen (FiO2) was 0.4 via a nasal cannula
High flow rate was decreased to 20 L/min
Biphasic positive airway pressure (BiPAP) was applied only during sleep
